inertia_kgm2.description: Rotational inertia referred to this map's shaft, kg·m².

kind.description: Source kind — determines how the topology consumes this map.

mass_kg.description: Mass attributed to this unit, kg.

schema.description: Schema version, e.g. `ptm/1.0` (`ptm/1.1` adds the optional Vdc axis).

tables.description: Sidecar table references and column semantics.

LoadAxis.description:
The load-axis declaration: absolute torque or a normalized fraction.

Externally tagged, so the wire form is `{torque_nm: [...]}` **or** `{load_fraction: [...]}`.
A load fraction is in −1..1 where negative values are the regen quadrant.

MapRef.description: Reference to a numeric sidecar map table (parquet/CSV), e.g. an aero or K&C map.

Overload.description: Time-limited overload envelope: torque curves valid for each listed duration.

durations_s.description: Durations for which the paired curves are valid, s.

PtmAxes.description: The axes of a powertrain map.

load_axis.description: Load-axis kind — either an absolute torque axis or a normalized load fraction.

torque_nm.description: Torque axis, N·m (the sampled torque grid, paired with `speed_rpm`).

vdc_v.description:
Optional DC-link voltage axis, V (ascending) — `ptm/1.1`. When present, the sidecar tables
carry a `vdc_v` column and the map is a 3-D `(speed_rpm, torque_nm, vdc_v)` tensor evaluated
at the pack's SoC-dependent terminal voltage (the Vdc–SoC coupling, §8.4). When absent the
map is single-voltage (measured at the scalar [`PtmMeta::dc_voltage_v`]).

PtmKind.description: Source kind of a powertrain map.

oneOf.description: A bare electric machine (torque available at its own shaft; a downstream ratio may apply).

A machine+inverter+gearbox lumped at the wheel-side shaft. The topology must **not** apply
another gear ratio unless [`PtmMeta::upstream_ratio_applied`] is `false`.

PtmLimits.description:
Limit envelopes.

Only `max_torque_nm_vs_speed` (the peak envelope) is required. `cont_torque_nm_vs_speed` and
`overload`, when present, serve as **validation references**, not the derating mechanism —
thermal capability is computed by the `.emotor` model from the loss tables (Decision #25).

cont_torque_nm_vs_speed.description: Continuous torque vs speed, N·m (optional validation reference).

drag_torque_nm_vs_speed.description:
Drag/braking torque vs speed, N·m (optional). This is *parasitic* drag (engine pumping,
spin losses) — not the commandable regen envelope, which is `max_regen_torque_nm_vs_speed`.

max_regen_torque_nm_vs_speed.description:
Peak **regenerative braking** torque vs speed, N·m as a **positive magnitude** (`ptm/1.2`).
This is the machine's negative-quadrant capability, the ceiling a regen brake blend may draw
on before the friction brakes fill the deficit.

When absent, the machine is assumed **symmetric** — the regen envelope equals
`max_torque_nm_vs_speed`. That is the usual first-order truth for a PMSM/IM whose limit is set
by inverter current and flux weakening rather than by quadrant, and it is surfaced as an
*estimated* value in the loaded-model report. Declare this curve whenever the real machine is
asymmetric (many road drive units are, to protect the DC link).

overload.description: Overload envelope by duration (optional validation reference).

PtmMeta.description: Free-form provenance/metadata for a powertrain map.

dc_voltage_v.description: DC bus voltage the map was measured at, V (electric machines).

upstream_ratio_applied.description:
For `drive_unit` maps: whether the internal gear ratio is already applied (default `true`).
When `false`, the topology may apply one downstream ratio.

PtmTables.description: Sidecar table reference plus the column semantics it must carry.

efficiency.description: Efficiency column present (0..1, covering drive and regen quadrants).

loss_w.description: Whether a `loss_w` column is present (must be consistent with `efficiency` if both given).

SchemaVersion.description: Schema version of the form <name>/<MAJOR>.<MINOR>, e.g. vehicle/1.0

TorqueCurve.description: A torque-vs-speed curve: paired equal-length speed/torque arrays.
